creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Baptiste Daroussin from comment #3)

The port already does @sample %%ETCDIR%%/screenrc.sample

There are two options. Option 1 is to install the screenrc that is provided by the upline. Option 2 is to install the screenrc as decided in D1289.

Regardless of the option the upline version is always installed in ${EXAMPLESDIR}/screenrc.sample-1 and the D1289 version is installed in ${EXAMPLESDIR}/screenrc.sample-2.

The default is to install the D1289 version, which is installed by the package. This PR requests to reverse the policy to install the upline version by default. The issue I see here is that we will undoubtedly receive another PR to switch it back. No matter which choice we make, someone will be unhappy. I think we need a consensus to avoid groundhog day.

post-install:
        @${MKDIR} ${STAGEDIR}${EXAMPLESDIR}
        ${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/etc/etcscreenrc \
                ${STAGEDIR}${EXAMPLESDIR}/screenrc.sample-1
        ${INSTALL_DATA} ${FILESDIR}/screenrc.sample \
                ${STAGEDIR}${EXAMPLESDIR}/screenrc.sample-2
        @${MKDIR} ${STAGEDIR}${ETCDIR}
.if ${PORT_OPTIONS:MSYSTEM_SCREENRC}
        ${INSTALL_DATA} ${FILESDIR}/screenrc.sample \
                ${STAGEDIR}${ETCDIR}/screenrc.sample
.else
        ${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/etc/etcscreenrc \
                ${STAGEDIR}${ETCDIR}/screenrc.sample
.endif
